text: Code reviews are a vital part of software development. During a code review, team members examine each other's code before it is added to the main project. This process is excellent for catching bugs early, when they are much simpler and cheaper to fix. More importantly, code reviews promote knowledge sharing. When developers read and comment on each other's work, they learn new techniques and understand different parts of the project. This shared understanding reduces the risk of problems if someone is unavailable. Ultimately, consistent code reviews build a higher standard of team quality, creating better software and stronger, more collaborative developers.
